message:
feat(c-api): implement multi-statement batch execution and real column names in sqlite3_exec

sqlite3_exec previously routed the entire SQL string through a single
Connection::query() call, which only executed the last statement and
returned its rows. Real SQLite iterates over semicolon-delimited
statements, executing each in turn and invoking the callback for every
result row.

The new execute_exec_batch() function loops over statements using
first_statement_tail_offset() (wrapping parse_first_statement_with_tail)
to split the batch, executes each statement individually via
conn.query(), and feeds rows to the callback between statements. Parse
errors in later statements report their offset relative to the original
full SQL string, matching SQLite's behavior.

The exec callback now receives actual column names instead of synthetic
"column0", "column1" placeholders. best_effort_exec_callback_column_names()
parses the statement and calls conn.prepare() to extract real column
metadata. The lookup is best-effort: failures log a warning and fall
back to the indexed names.

The ExecCallback type alias consolidates the verbose function pointer
signature that was previously duplicated in the extern declaration and
inline callback code.

New tests cover:
- Column name fidelity (SELECT 1 AS alpha, 2 AS beta)
- Multi-statement batch with per-statement callback rows
- Parse error offset reporting for failures in later batch statements
- Tail statement preparation after sqlite3_prepare_v2

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
